user_message: Just got this from Patricia Kim at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery -- I need help figuring out whitespace analysis. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 15:08 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery whitespace analysis

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-28 14:05 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 15:24 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with whitespace analysis. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-N trap (Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-N failure mode (Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-N -- Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
